outline: Trig ratios of acute and special angles

modules.title: Module 1 · Trig Ratios
modules.html:
In a right-angled triangle with an acute angle $\theta$:

$$\sin\theta=\dfrac{\text{opp}}{\text{hyp}},\quad \cos\theta=\dfrac{\text{adj}}{\text{hyp}},\quad \tan\theta=\dfrac{\text{opp}}{\text{adj}}$$

Special angles: $\sin 30° = \tfrac{1}{2}$, $\cos 30° = \tfrac{\sqrt 3}{2}$, $\tan 45° = 1$, $\sin 60° = \tfrac{\sqrt 3}{2}$.

modules.title: Module 2 · Elevation & Depression
modules.html: The angle of elevation is measured upward from the horizontal to the line of sight of a higher object. The angle of depression is measured downward from horizontal to a lower object. Both use the same value between the horizontal and the sight line.

examples.q: From a point 20 m from a tower, the angle of elevation to the top is $30°$. Find the height of the tower.

modules.title: Module 3 · Bearings
modules.html:
Three-figure bearings are measured clockwise from North (always 3 digits: $045°$, $270°$).

Compass bearings use directions like N40°E (start from N or S, rotate through the given angle towards E or W).

examples.q: A boat sails N60°E for 100 km. What is its bearing in three-figure notation?
